results.claim: Cumulative E2E speedup is 1.0156x after Round 1
One optimization (mla_gemv) was shipped in Round 1
MLA q_b_proj optimization delivered -1.56% E2E improvement at BS=1
Correctness verified as bitwise identical (max_abs_err = 0.0) across all tested M values
Implementation was 91 lines in a single Python file (mla.py)
Pipeline benchmark showed only 1.20x speedup at BS=1, with BS=8 regression at 0.84x
Root cause: modular GEMV requires 5+ separate kernel launches vs TRT-LLM single fused launch
Debate terminated early after 1 round because all positions were well-developed and no new evidence would emerge

Champion-3 scored highest (7.70) with strongest empirical evidence: 8.5x measured speedup under CUDA graph + cold-cache, lowest implementation risk (~250 LOC), and unique MLA target orthogonal to MoE proposals
Winner selection ranked Champion-3 first (7.70), Champion-2 second (6.65), Champion-1 third (5.80)
Delegate-3a confirmed kv_b_proj is absorbed in decode -- new information not in bottleneck_analysis.md
Delegate-3a identified mla.py:139 as the exact integration intercept
Delegate-1a confirmed B200 L2 cache = 126 MB via live hardware query
Delegate-2a provided wrong BMM attribution (shared expert 'most likely') that champion-2 used without correction
Delegate-1a reported W2 per-rank shape as [256, 768, 2048] -- K and N are transposed vs the correct [256, 6144, 256]
Champion-1 claimed f_decode(BS=8) ~8-12% and E2E ~6.4-9.6% without any BS=8 measurement
Champion-1 conceded BS=8 estimates were speculative with no measurement basis
Champion-2 attributed FlashInfer BMM 18.8% to shared expert code path and built Target A on it
Champion-2 conceded the code path misidentification in rebuttal after adversarial critique
Champion-1 claimed spin-wait compression could amplify E2E gain by 2x without any measurement data
Champion-2's final revised E2E projection was 2.7-3.2% at BS=1
Validator projected only 0.39% E2E improvement -- well below the 1.5% kill criterion
Delegate-1a had confirmed TRTLLM-GEN IS fully fused during debate -- this info was available but not incorporated into E2E projections
Stage 2 profiling was BS=1 only -- only baseline_bs1_node.nsys-rep captured successfully
Round 2 debate team was named and configured but produced no results
Primary failure reason: pipeline-level speedup (1.20x) insufficient to meet E2E kill criterion due to monolithic kernel fusion advantage
Lesson 2: Dispatch-level optimization can be more impactful than kernel replacement -- 91 lines delivered 1.56% E2E with zero correctness risk
Adversarial debate forced champion-2 to concede code path misidentification
Champion-3 cited delegate-3a's finding and scoped target to q_b_proj only
Lesson 1: Monolithic kernel fusion trumps modular kernel speedup -- raw 2.44x GEMV became only 1.20x at pipeline level
Amdahl's Law prediction validated within 1.14x of measured result on B200 hardware
Profiling under production parity: CUDA graphs (PIECEWISE) + torch.compile with norm_quant, act_quant fusions active
nsys trace captured 40,456 kernel instances across 8 TP ranks
Communication accounts for 83.2% of total GPU time, with 227.5 ms non-communication compute
B200 hardware confirmed: sm_100 CC 10.0, 183359 MiB total memory, 1965 MHz max SM clock
